behavior: Active foliage gleaner; rapidly hops and flits through branches, taking insects and spiders from leaves and twig tips.
field_marks: Tiny, compact warbler with a short tail and thin, pointed bill.
Blue-gray head and upperparts with a yellow-green patch centered on the back.
Two crisp white wingbars on dark wings.
Bright yellow throat and upper breast contrasting with a whitish belly.
Pale white eye crescents are conspicuous.
Adult male typically shows a dark and chestnut breast band; females and immature birds are paler and usually lack it.
habitat: Mature, humid deciduous or coniferous forest, especially along streams, swamps, and other wet lowlands with hanging mosses or lichens; also uses wooded and scrubby habitats outside the breeding season.

sources.title: Northern Parula Life History | All About Birds
